QF.id: Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_12

question.text: A Specialized Investment Fund (SIF), recently introduced by SEBI, requires a minimum investment of:

answer.text: Rs. 10 lakh per investor across all SIF strategies in one AMC
Rs. 1 crore per investor, similar to AIF Category III
Rs. 50 lakh per investor, similar to PMS
Rs. 25 lakh per investor per strategy

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SEBI introduced the Specialized Investment Fund (SIF) as a new product category positioned between regular mutual funds and PMS/AIF. The minimum investment requirement is Rs. 10 lakh per investor across all SIF strategies within a single AMC. SIFs can employ strategies like long-short equity, inverse funds, and other sophisticated approaches not available in regular MF schemes.
**The Trap:** This is a classic "Cluster Confusion" question mixing minimum investment thresholds across products. Rs. 1 crore is the minimum for AIF (Category I, II, III). Rs. 50 lakh is the minimum for PMS. Rs. 25 lakh is a made-up figure. The hierarchy is: MF (Rs. 500 SIP) < SIF (Rs. 10 lakh) < PMS (Rs. 50 lakh) < AIF (Rs. 1 crore).
**Cross-Exam Context:** In NISM III-C (Investment Adviser), the comparison across MF, PMS, and AIF minimum investments is a key topic. NISM V-B may test SIF as a new development. The positioning of SIF fills the gap between MFs and PMS, offering sophisticated strategies to informed investors at a lower threshold than PMS.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SIF is an opportunity to serve HNI clients who want MF-like convenience with PMS-like strategies. Position it to clients with Rs. 10 lakh+ investable surplus who are looking for differentiated strategies beyond vanilla MF categories. However, ensure the client understands the higher complexity and risk involved.
